customer_anxiety_profile: Low overt anxiety but structurally uncertain: household is mid-move into a new home, has no bill history, and credit qualification is unresolved. Fannie is the primary contact for the appointment; Sam is on title but was unavailable during the call. The household is in a major life transition — receptive but potentially distracted.
unresolved_concerns: Credit score unknown for both Sam and Fannie — neither could confirm qualification. Rep reassured on the 650 threshold (note: schema specifies 670; threshold discrepancy unresolved at floor level) without actual score data.
No bill history available — household just moved in. Closer will need to work from estimated load or utility account setup rather than historical bill data.
Appointment framed as 'drop by' — if this is a phone consultation, the framing needs correction before the appointment date to avoid confusion about format.
sensitive_topics_raised: Fannie referenced her elderly father qualifying for solar 'out of his age' — implies familiarity with solar programs through a family member, and may carry expectations about age-based qualification paths. Closer should be prepared to address this without over-promising.

Deepgram transcription artifact: the credit threshold '650' is rendered as '$6.50' in the transcript. This is a speech-to-text error — the rep stated '650,' not a dollar figure. Do not interpret as a dollar amount.
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will 'drop by' to show the design and answer questions — closer should confirm whether this is phone or in-person and align with Fannie before the appointment date
Implied that credit around 650 'should be good' — closer should not contradict this without a soft reframe
closer_should_know: Both Sam and Fannie are on title and Fannie confirmed both will be available Saturday at 10AM — that's the structural win. The risk is that credit is genuinely unknown and the household is mid-move with no bill history. Lead the appointment with curiosity about the new home ('what made you choose this house?') rather than bill pain — they don't have a bill yet, but they have a major financial decision in front of them. The new-home framing is the pitch lever the setter didn't use.
